title: Queenstown vs Wanaka for Families — Which Should You Choose?
meta_desc: Compare Queenstown and Wanaka for family travel in NZ, considering cost, activities and vibe
intro: When planning a family trip to the South Island, two popular destinations often come to mind: Queenstown and Wanaka. While both offer stunning natural beauty and plenty of activities, they cater to different needs and preferences. With accommodation prices in Queenstown starting from $200 per night, it's essential to choose the right town for your family's budget and interests.

sections.heading: Cost Comparison
sections.body: Accommodation is one of the most significant expenses for families. In Queenstown, you can expect to pay around $250-350 per night for a family-friendly hotel or apartment. In contrast, Wanaka offers more affordable options, with prices starting from $180 per night for a similar standard of accommodation.

sections.heading: Activities for Families
list: Skiing and snowboarding at Coronet Peak ($149 per adult, $99 per child aged 6-17) or Cardrona ($159 per adult, $109 per child aged 6-17)
Hiking the Ben Lomond Track in Queenstown (free) or the Mount Iron Track in Wanaka (free)
Visiting the Skyline Gondola in Queenstown ($44 per adult, $26 per child aged 6-17) or the Wanaka Tree ($0, but donations welcome)

sections.heading: Vibe and Atmosphere
sections.body: Queenstown has a more vibrant and lively atmosphere, with a wider range of restaurants, bars, and shops. Wanaka, on the other hand, is generally quieter and more laid-back, making it an excellent choice for families seeking a relaxing holiday.

sections.heading: Getting Around
sections.body: The drive between Queenstown and Wanaka takes approximately 1 hour, depending on road conditions. If you prefer not to drive, you can take a bus or shuttle service, such as the InterCity bus ($20-$30 per person, one way) or the TrackNet shuttle ($35-$45 per person, one way).

sections.heading: Ages and Interests
list: Families with young children (under 6): Wanaka's quieter atmosphere and more affordable accommodation options make it an excellent choice
Families with teenagers: Queenstown's wider range of activities, including skiing, snowboarding, and adventure sports, may be more appealing
Families with older children: Consider the specific interests of your children and choose the destination that best aligns with their preferences
